title: Baseline Update Documentation — Config Baseline Version Changes

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-010-4 R1 Part 1.3.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-010-4 R1 Part 1.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.56: Baseline Update Documentation — Config Baseline Version Changes.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.56' running on index=grc sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version earliest=-365d
| sort 0 - _time
| streamstats current=f latest(baseline_version) as prev_version by platform
| where baseline_version!=prev_version
| eval has_auth=if(isnotnull(auth_record_id),1,0)
| where has_auth=0
| table _time, platform, baseline_version, prev_version, owner, auth_record_id
description: We audit when official configuration baseline versions change and require an authorizing record for each update.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes cip010_baseline_version, cip010_baseline_version`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Emit version bump events from CMDB or GitOps. (2) Require `auth_record_id` from CAB approval. (3) Alert compliance on missing linkage. (4) Archive prior baselines offline. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`, `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version``.
visualization: Timeline (version bumps), Table (missing auth), Single value (violations). Key fields: `auth_record_id`, `baseline_version`, `has_auth`, `owner`, `platform`, `prev_version`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit version bump events from CMDB or GitOps. (2) Require `auth_record_id` from CAB approval. (3) Alert compliance on missing linkage. (4) Archive prior baselines offline.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version earliest=-365d
| sort 0 - _time
| streamstats current=f latest(baseline_version) as prev_version by platform
| where baseline_version!=prev_version
| eval has_auth=if(isnotnull(auth_record_id),1,0)
| where has_auth=0
| table _time, platform, baseline_version, prev_version, owner, auth_record_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Baseline Update Documentation — Config Baseline Version Changes (CIP-010-4 R1 Part 1.3)** — We audit when official configuration baseline versions change and require an authorizing record for each update.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: cip010_baseline_version. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by platform** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where baseline_version!=prev_version` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_auth** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where has_auth=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, auth_record_id, baseline_version, has_auth, owner, platform, prev_version.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (version bumps), Table (missing auth), Single value (violations)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we audit when official configuration baseline versions change and require an authorizing record for each update. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
